lead: Post-close 2026-07-20: bounce fails as Houthi blockade extends the war premium and yields press the July high

sources.use: Houthi naval blockade declared against Saudi Arabia; page rendered and captured on screen (visuals/02-usnews-blockade.mp4) — Reuters byline, July 20 2026, 8:09 a.m.

Ninth consecutive day of US strikes; talks collapsed over the Hormuz MOU; third US service member killed since Friday; CENTCOM anti-shipping targeting. Page rendered and captured (visuals/02b-npr-strikes.mp4).
Corroborates the blockade declaration and the ~4.5M bpd Red Sea (Yanbu) workaround volume. Search-result sourced; not independently fetched.
Crude ~+20% July MTD; Brent above $90 intraday. Fetch attempts returned 403; claim carried as single_source with on-air attribution.
Hormuz chokepoint baseline (~20M b/d, ~20% of global petroleum liquids, 2024) and Q1 2026 flow 14.6M b/d (-30% y/y).

Goldman April 9 scenario: Brent >$100 if Hormuz disruption persists another month. Spoken only as a dated scenario, attributed. URL is the best-fit candidate from search results; the date and figures are cross-checked in the fact pack.
FOMC blackout Jul 18-30; decision Jul 29 14:00 ET.

unverified_banned: Baghaei diplomacy signal (CNBC live blog) — could not be pinned to a fetchable page; DROPPED from script; intraday oil fade narrated as price action only
Web closing prints for NDX/US10Y/DXY/WTI/Brent — conflicting across outlets; superseded by TradingView feed reads per doctrine (chart = SoT for levels)
Apr 9 'Brent >$100 on Monday' line — different Monday, never a Jul 20 fact
